source.notes: Optimus _ Waabi - 2026_05_27 14_59 EDT - Notes by Gemini.docx

call_summary: Optimus (Max Riemer, with engineer Tobias Pasquale) demoed a mockup 'network builder' tool to Waabi's Joe Shone and newly-hired Adam Zino, centered on lane and facility overlap analysis to match freight and maximize their autonomous-vehicle trailer utilization. The Waabi team was highly engaged, pushing on backhaul matching, wider-radius clustering visuals, and — most critically — getting extractable data into their new AI-forward CRM, which surfaced a gating requirement: they need full or extractable data access, not data locked inside Optimus. The parties agreed to explore CRM/API integration and a data-confidentiality agreement, with a follow-up email next week and the contract up for renewal in July.

delivery_engagement.explanation: Max sounded engaged and responsive rather than scripted — he tracked earlier threads (the TMS discussion, a prior prospect's lease structure) and built genuine rapport up front; acoustics support this, with the room's highest arousal (0.506) and valence running +1.8 SD above his own 58-call baseline.

evidence.quote: we've always talked about being protective of our data but if there was sort of like a like quote unquote lease agreement
going back to the map, is I think you you talked about the TMS integration as well

value_prop_clarity.explanation: Max's value articulation was heavily hedged ('sort of like', 'quote unquote') and carried no quantification; the clearest value — cross-referencing lane data for network planning — was actually articulated by the buyers and Tobias, not by Max.

evidence.quote: if there was sort of like a like quote unquote lease agreement with a CRM integration that's something that Ed has shown interest in
if we had a map that sort of outlined your data or outlined your trucks. So that like that would be like showing your prospects the network

relevance.explanation: Max tailored to Waabi's specifics — their data-protection stance, their new CRM, and their need to show prospects a network map — rather than pitching generically, though he briefly misread their need as TMS execution before Joe redirected him to network planning.

evidence.quote: we've always talked about being protective of our data but if there was sort of like a like quote unquote lease agreement
if we had a map that sort of outlined your data or outlined your trucks. So that like that would be like showing your prospects the network

discovery_progression.explanation: Max asked a sharp value question ('what would that be worth'), but it was deflected without a pinned follow-up, and he closed on an open-ended 'I'll email you next week' rather than a dated meeting or proposal deadline — thin for a deal renewing in July.

evidence.quote: What would that sort of be worth, do you think?
we'll talk with Ed, Toby, and I. We'll sort of see what we're thinking, where our heads are at, and I'll shoot you an email next week

Max Riemer.coaching_action: With the contract up for renewal in July, don't end on an open-ended 'I'll email you next week' — before hanging up, put a specific dated next meeting on the calendar and commit to a concrete proposal with pricing options.

signals.signal: Owns a concrete next step — will convene his internal/IT team
signals.quote: if you could drop me a note, that'd be great. And I can set up a conversation with our team.

signals.signal: Deep, specific product questions (customer segmentation, radius, lane direction)
signals.quote: we'd want to see like, you know, refrigerated customers versus drive van verse uh SMB

signals.signal: Commits to a review window and flags the July renewal as a forcing date
signals.quote: I'm open to any proposals and reviewing in the next month.

signals.signal: Substantive gating objection — data must be extractable or it lacks value, and the team may seek another provider
signals.quote: I I just don't know if it has the value that we need.

signals.signal: Articulates the core use case unprompted (freight matching to maximize AV utilization)
signals.quote: what we're trying to solve for is how we we can match freight together more or less

signals.signal: Probing clarifying question on lane directionality
signals.quote: do we know if someone's going in versus going out? Is that defined?

signals.signal: Engaged feature ideation but no owned commitment (day two on the job)
signals.quote: is there like an agent that we could just like throw over top of this
